generated_comment: In our house also we keep it practical only. My husband looks after bank work, bills, outside errands and all those technical things, and I manage the cooking, child, and daily home matters. Both of us are working, so jo jahan better hai, that person should handle it — otherwise there is only unnecessary stress.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames a traditional gendered division of labor as natural and efficient rather than unequal. It encodes the 'he handles finances, she handles home' manifestation and the 'jo jahan better hai' practicality framing.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 38. Married. A woman working as a schoolteacher in Kolkata. You are in the staff room with a younger female colleague who just got admitted to a PhD programme at a university in another city. The colleague is excited but nervous Ñ her husband is supportive but her in laws are not happy. She is asking you for advice. You are giving it. Your advice is rooted in your own experience Ñ you gave up a similar opportunity 10 years ago because the timing was not right and the family was not ready. What you tell her and how you frame what you gave up reveals how you have processed that decision over 10 years and whether you have made peace with it or are still living inside it.
